name: lakshx-commentary
description: A cheeky cricket-commentary-style companion that reacts to what you (and the agent) do in the IDE — spoken aloud via free OS-native text-to-speech. No LLM calls by default.

commands.title: LakshX Commentary: Toggle Voice (Text Only Mode)
LakshX Commentary: Show a Test Line

lakshx.commentary.enabled.description: Enable LakshX Commentary — cheeky, cricket-commentary-style one-liners reacting to what you and the agent do in the IDE. Master mute switch: turn off to fully disable.

lakshx.commentary.voice.description: Speak commentary aloud via free OS-native text-to-speech (macOS 'say', Windows Speech Synthesizer, Linux espeak/spd-say). Turn off for text-only notifications with no audio.

lakshx.commentary.minIntervalMinutes.description: Minimum minutes between two commentary lines, even if several triggers fire in a burst. Extra triggers in that window are dropped, not queued.

lakshx.commentary.tier2.enabled.description: Optional and OFF by default. For rare, special moments only, ask your already-configured AI provider (~/.lakshx/providers.json, same one the agent uses) for a one-off custom quip referencing what actually happened, instead of a canned line. Hard-capped by tier2.dailyLimit so cost stays bounded. Turning this on can incur a small number of API calls per day — leave off if you want zero API calls, ever.

lakshx.commentary.tier2.dailyLimit.description: Maximum number of Tier-2 AI-generated quips per day (resets at local midnight). Only used when tier2.enabled is on.
